title: Immigrant Rights Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Immigrant Rights Groups 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CATHOLIC CHARITIES IMMIGRATION LEGAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides professional legal representation, advice, and advocacy to low-income immigrants to protect and advance their rights. Focuses on family reunification and assistance to the most vulnerable immigrants, including victims of crimes such as domestic violence and human trafficking. Engages in public education, training, and community outreach to inform immigrants about their rights and options for immigration relief. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard #5 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Low-income immigrants Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm by appointment only Email (503) 231-4866 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND IMMIGRANT RIGHTS COALITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and services to protect and empower the immigrant community including a hotline to report local Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) sightings, assistance with locating loved ones who have been detained or deported, education on immigrant rights including free Know Your Rights fliers and handouts, drop-in resource center, and information and referral to additional resources such as legal aid or accompaniment to check-ins at the ICE office. Also offers volunteer opportunities. 4122 S Moody Avenue Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 7:45am-noon Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 622-1510 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development. Promotes systemic changes in many issues including affordable housing, fair wages, racial justice, etc. by empowering communities and grassroots organizing. Does not provide direct advocacy for individuals. 1390 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Immigrant and refugee community members and non-immigrant community members Hours: Varies Email (503) 287-4117 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON UNITE OREGON ROGUE VALLEY CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development. Promotes systemic changes in many issues including affordable housing, fair wages, racial justice, etc. by empowering communities and grassroots organizing. Does not provide direct advocacy for individuals. 1390 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Immigrant and refugee community members and non-immigrant community members Hours: Varies Email (541) 772-4029 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON UNITE OREGON WASHINGTON COUNTY CIVIC ENGAGEMENT AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Protects and expands the rights of immigrant, refugee, and underserved communities by offering community organizing, policy advocacy, community education, and leadership development. Promotes systemic changes in many issues including affordable housing, fair wages, racial justice, etc. by empowering communities and grassroots organizing.
